title: Calonema uliginosum
intro: Calonema uliginosum, also known as marsh violet or marsh violas, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Violaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating skin conditions and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Calonema uliginosum include flavonoids, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Calonema uliginosum is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly consumed for their purported health benefits and used in traditional folk medicine.
taxonomy: Calonema uliginosum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Calonema, which is a part of the subtribe Coelogyneinae. The plant is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Vietnam, Thailand, and Malaysia. Calonema uliginosum is an epiphytic orchid, meaning it grows on other plants or trees. It is typically found in humid, shaded areas and is known for its small, fragrant flowers.
